title_en: Deception Among Rulers, the Wealthy, and Common People
content_en:
Talbis Iblis is not solely a critique of religious scholars and spiritual practitioners. Ibn al-Jawzi devotes significant attention to the ways Satan deceives rulers, wealthy people, and ordinary Muslims — demonstrating that the phenomenon of religious self-deception operates across every stratum of society.

For rulers and political leaders, the primary form of deception identified by Ibn al-Jawzi is the confusion between power and piety. A ruler who believes that his political success reflects divine favor — and uses this belief to justify injustice, excessive wealth, or the suppression of legitimate religious criticism — is in the grip of a subtle but catastrophic self-deception. The Quran's stories of Pharaoh and other tyrants serve as Ibn al-Jawzi's evidence: these figures were not irreligious in their self-understanding, but religious language became in their hands a tool for self-justification rather than a standard against which to measure themselves.

The deception of wealth is similarly subtle. A wealthy person who gives charity, funds mosques, and makes visible contributions to the religious community can believe themselves to be especially favored by God — while simultaneously committing injustice in their business dealings, failing to uphold their family obligations, or using their philanthropy primarily to purchase social status. Ibn al-Jawzi's analysis of the wealthy person's spiritual danger echoes the prophetic warnings about wealth — 'I do not fear poverty for you; I fear that the world will be opened for you as it was for those before you' — while grounding the critique in specific behavioral patterns.

For ordinary Muslims, the forms of deception are more mundane but equally dangerous. Ibn al-Jawzi describes the person who performs the visible rituals of Islam — prayer, fasting, pilgrimage — without the inner transformation these acts are meant to produce. The external act of prayer is complete, but the heart is absent. The fast is observed, but the tongue and eyes and hands fast from nothing. The pilgrimage is made, but the traveler returns unchanged. Satan's deception in these cases is to let the external shell of religiosity stand in for its substance — to allow the person to feel righteous without becoming righteous.

He also addresses the common failure of prioritizing minor religious acts over major obligations. A person who is meticulous about the sunnan (recommended acts) of wudu while habitually neglecting the rights of their spouse or employees is exhibiting a characteristic form of Satanic confusion about what God actually requires. The fiqh of priorities — knowing which acts are obligatory and which are recommended, and ensuring the obligatory ones are performed before the recommended — is itself an Islamic science, and its neglect is fertile ground for the kind of religious performance that substitutes for genuine obedience.

Through these varied portraits, Ibn al-Jawzi builds a comprehensive picture of the human vulnerability to self-deception in matters of religion. The antidote he proposes is consistent throughout: measure all religious practice against the Quran, the authentic Sunnah, and the scholarly consensus of the mainstream Sunni tradition. Not against one's own feelings of spiritual elevation, not against the opinions of a charismatic spiritual guide whose authority is claimed but not established, and not against the approval of the religious community one moves in. The standard is the revealed text, carefully transmitted and correctly understood.
title_id: Tipuan di antara Para Penguasa, Orang Kaya, dan Orang Awam
content_id:
Talbis Iblis bukan semata kritik terhadap para ulama agama dan praktisi spiritual. Ibn al-Jawzi memberikan perhatian signifikan pada cara-cara Setan menipu penguasa, orang kaya, dan Muslim biasa — menunjukkan bahwa fenomena penipuan diri agama beroperasi di setiap lapisan masyarakat.

Untuk penguasa dan pemimpin politik, bentuk utama tipuan yang diidentifikasi Ibn al-Jawzi adalah kebingungan antara kekuasaan dan kesalehan. Seorang penguasa yang percaya bahwa keberhasilan politiknya mencerminkan karunia ilahi — dan menggunakan keyakinan ini untuk membenarkan ketidakadilan, kekayaan berlebihan, atau penindasan terhadap kritik keagamaan yang sah — berada dalam cengkeraman penipuan diri yang halus tetapi dahsyat. Kisah-kisah Al-Qur'an tentang Fir'aun dan para tiran lainnya melayani sebagai bukti Ibn al-Jawzi: tokoh-tokoh ini tidak jahat dalam pemahaman diri mereka, tetapi bahasa agama menjadi di tangan mereka alat untuk membenarkan diri daripada standar untuk mengukur diri.

Tipuan kekayaan juga halus. Orang kaya yang bersedekah, mendanai masjid, dan membuat kontribusi yang terlihat kepada komunitas keagamaan dapat percaya diri bahwa mereka sangat dikasihi Allah — sementara secara bersamaan melakukan ketidakadilan dalam urusan bisnis mereka, gagal memenuhi kewajiban keluarga mereka, atau menggunakan filantropi mereka terutama untuk membeli status sosial.

Untuk Muslim biasa, bentuk-bentuk tipuan lebih biasa tetapi sama berbahayanya. Ibn al-Jawzi menggambarkan orang yang melaksanakan ritual-ritual Islam yang terlihat — salat, puasa, haji — tanpa transformasi batin yang seharusnya dihasilkan oleh tindakan-tindakan ini. Tindakan lahiriah salat lengkap, tetapi hati tidak hadir. Puasa dipatuhi, tetapi lisan dan mata dan tangan tidak berpuasa dari apa pun. Haji dilakukan, tetapi pelancong kembali tidak berubah. Tipuan Setan dalam kasus-kasus ini adalah membiarkan cangkang lahiriah keagamaan berdiri sebagai pengganti substansinya — membiarkan seseorang merasa saleh tanpa menjadi saleh.

Beliau juga membahas kegagalan umum dalam memprioritaskan ibadah-ibadah kecil di atas kewajiban-kewajiban besar. Seseorang yang teliti tentang sunnah-sunnah wudu sementara secara teratur mengabaikan hak-hak pasangan atau karyawan mereka menunjukkan bentuk khas kebingungan Setan tentang apa yang sebenarnya Allah tuntut. Fikih prioritas — mengetahui tindakan mana yang wajib dan mana yang sunnah, dan memastikan yang wajib dilakukan sebelum yang sunnah — adalah ilmu Islam itu sendiri, dan pengabaiannya adalah lahan subur bagi jenis penampilan keagamaan yang menggantikan ketaatan yang tulus.

Melalui berbagai potret ini, Ibn al-Jawzi membangun gambaran komprehensif tentang kerentanan manusia terhadap penipuan diri dalam hal-hal agama. Penawarnya konsisten di seluruh: ukur semua praktik keagamaan terhadap Al-Qur'an, Sunnah yang otentik, dan konsensus ilmiah tradisi Sunni arus utama.
